vancode/interpreter/value

      • `$`(value: Value): string
        foreign

        initArray

        initObject
      • initObject(id: TypeId; fieldCount: int): Value
        initValue
      • initValue(nptr: pointer; libpath: string): Value
      • initValue(v: bool): Value
      • initValue(v: float64): Value
      • initValue(v: int64): Value
      • initValue(v: JsonNode): Value

        toString
      • toString(value: JsonNode): string

This module defines the Value type, which represents a value in the interpreter. This represents all the possible values that can be manipulated by the interpreter, including primitive types like bools and ints, as well as complex types like objects and arrays.

It also includes utilities for initializing and converting values, as well as a foreign proc system for handling native code in the standard library and user-defined foreign procs in general.

Types

ForeignProc = proc (args: StackView; argc: int): Value
A foreign proc implementation, used for native code in the standard library and user-defined foreign procs in general 
HtmlObject = object
Object {.acyclic.} = ref object
  case isForeign*: bool
  of true:
    data*: pointer           ## the foreign data pointer
    libpath*: string         ## the path to the dynamic library this foreign object came from
  of false:
    keys*: seq[string]       ## the field names of this object
                             ## stored in the same order as `fields`
    fields*: seq[Value]      ## the fields of this object, stored
                             ## in the same order as `keys`
A hayago object. 
Stack = seq[Value]
A runtime stack of values, used in the VM. 
StackView = ptr UncheckedArray[Value]
An unsafe view into a Stack. 
TypeId = range[0 .. 32766]
Value {.acyclic.} = ref object
  case typeId*: TypeId       ## the type ID, used for dynamic dispatch
  of tyBool:
    boolVal*: bool
  of tyInt:
    intVal*: int64
  of tyFloat:
    floatVal*: float64
  of tyString:
    stringVal*: ref string
  of tyJsonStorage:
    jsonVal*: JsonNode
  of tyHtmlObject:
    htmlObject*: HtmlObject
  else:
    objectVal*: Object
ValuePtr = Value
A pointer to a value.

Consts

nilObject = -1
The field count used for initializing a nil object. 
tyArrayObject = 12
tyBool = 1
tyFirstObject = 10
tyFloat = 3
tyHtmlObject = 13
tyInt = 2
tyJsonStorage = 11
tyNil = 0
tyPointer = 15
tyString = 4
ValueSize = 16

Procs

proc `$`(value: Value): string {....raises: [], tags: [RootEffect], forbids: [].}
Returns a value's string representation. 
proc foreign(value: Value; T: typedesc): T:type
Get an object value. This is a mostly safe operation, but attempting to get a foreign type different from the value's is undefined behavior. 
proc foreign(value: var Value; T: typedesc): T:type
proc initArray(length: int): Value {....raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
Initializes an array value. 
proc initObject(id: TypeId; fieldCount: int): Value {....raises: [], tags: [],
    forbids: [].}
Initializes a native object value, with fieldCount fields. 
proc initValue(nptr: pointer; libpath: string): Value {....raises: [], tags: [],
    forbids: [].}
Initializes a pointer value. 
proc initValue(v: bool): Value {....raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
Initializes a bool value. 
proc initValue(v: float64): Value {....raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
Initializes a float value. 
proc initValue(v: int64): Value {....raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
Initializes a float value. 
proc initValue(v: JsonNode): Value {....raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
Initializes a JSON value. 
proc initValue(v: string): Value {....raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
Initializes a string value. 
proc initValue[T: tuple | object | ref](id: TypeId; value: T): Value
Safely initializes a foreign object value. This copies the value onto the heap for ordinary objects and tuples, and GC_refs the value for refs. The finalizer for objectVal deallocates or GC_unrefs the foreign data to maintain memory safety. 
proc toString(value: JsonNode): string {....raises: [], tags: [RootEffect],
    forbids: [].}
Converts a JSON node to a string.
